channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Global tech sell-off deepens as South Korea's Kospi sinks 7%, S&P 500 futures fall: Live updates
item.description: The technology-dominant Nasdaq Composite and broad S&P 500 index are both on pace to end the trading week in the red.

item.title: SoftBank sinks 12% as Asia tech rout tracks declines in the U.S.
item.description: SoftBank Group plunged, leading a broad selloff in Asian technology stocks amid mounting concerns over the rising cost of artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: UN agency pauses Hormuz ship evacuation plan after first vessel attack under peace deal
item.description: The pause follows a container ship being struck near the coast of Oman on Thursday, with a U.S. official saying that Iran was responsible for the attack.

item.title: Oil slides nearly 2% as markets look past fresh Iran tensions and focus on supply outlook
item.description: Oil extended declines on Friday, with markets focused any further breakthroughs in the Middle East conflict, while digesting a flurry of news from the region.

item.title: Goldman says private credit may invest a lot of `dry powder' in this sector
item.description: Private credit may use its sizable amount of "dry powder" to combat potential financing market dislocations in this sector, according to Goldman Sachs.

item.title: Airwallex hits $11 billion valuation with $320 million raise as fintech pushes into finance run by AI agents
item.description: Airwallex raised $320 million to build the financial infrastructure powering a world where AI agents handle business payments and finance.

item.title: Ariel’s John Rogers likes these small-cap names as the market rally broadens
item.description: Ariel Investments' John Rogers highlighted for-profit education company Covista as a small-cap stock he's currently bullish on.

item.title: ON Semiconductor strikes $7 billion deal for Synaptics in physical AI push
item.description: ON Semiconductor said the deal bumps up its total addressable market by $30 billion, to $243 billion by 2030.

item.title: Iran behind attack on cargo vessel near Oman in Strait of Hormuz, U.S. official tells MS NOW
item.description: "The only crop we're harvesting is what you planted: decades of mistrust," Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said in a social media post.

item.title: Chicago Fed President Goolsbee says inflation is too high; Williams sees price pressures easing
item.description: In a live CNBC interview from his home district, Goolsbee declined to speculate on where he thinks interest rates are headed.

item.title: Rafael Nadal says he won't return to pro tennis following Serena Williams' comeback
item.description: Rafael Nadal told CNBC Sport he has closed the chapter on playing pro tennis forever.

item.title: Luxury spending now driven by experiences and 'inheritourism'
item.description: While luxury goods sales are expected to grow between 1% and 4%, experiences are on track for growth of between 3% and 7% this year, according to a new report.

item.title: Democrats probe Trump's troubled $16 million reflecting pool renovation
item.description: President Donald Trump has attempted to spruce up Washington in advance of the country's 250th anniversary.

item.title: Small-caps are booming this year. Here are some of Wall Street's top smaller picks
item.description: Small-cap stocks are on pace to notch their best first half of the year since 1991, and TD Cowen and Bank of America have ideas about how to play the trend.

item.title: Trump keeps turning Republican wins into loyalty tests — and political liabilities
item.description: President Donald Trump’s moves on housing, FISA, Iran and Washington projects are creating new headaches for Republicans trying to show voters they can govern.

item.title: Friday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
item.description: On Thursday, the Nasdaq Composite fell for a fourth straight day as shares of Apple and Microsoft slid.

item.title: Supreme Court limits Roundup cancer suits against Bayer's Monsanto
item.description: Glyphosate, used in Roundup weedkiller, is the most commonly utilized herbicide in agriculture, and it has long been linked to cancer claims.

item.title: Judge says lawsuit against Trump DOJ 'anti-weaponization' fund will proceed
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche originally set up the fund as part of a settlement of a lawsuit by President Donald Trump against the IRS.

item.title: GM reveals 2027 GMC Sierra pickup with new V-8 engines, redesigned styling
item.description: The new GMC Sierra trucks are crucial to GM's sales and earnings, especially highly profitable luxury Denali and off-road AT4 models.

item.title: As Social Security faces trust fund depletion, some Washington lawmakers call for taxing high earners
item.description: High earners may only contribute Social Security payroll taxes for part of the year. As the program faces funding woes, some lawmakers say that should change.

item.title: Apple stock gets slammed on bigger Mac, iPad price hikes. Why it can weather the storm
item.description: The stock had its worst session in more than a year as management made its first official move to pass higher memory costs onto consumers.

item.title: The best personal loans of July 2026
item.description: The top personal loan lenders cater to a range of needs, from poor credit to next-day funding.

item.title: Microsoft lifts price of Xbox consoles due to soaring component costs
item.description: Microsoft said it's increasing the price of Xbox game consoles after Apple announced price hikes for MacBooks and iPads.

item.title: JPMorgan names Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh co-presidents as longtime exec Marianne Lake exits
item.description: The moves would appear to make Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh the most obvious possible successors to JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon.

item.title: Judge blocks Trump's rule limiting federal student loans for certain grad school borrowers
item.description: A federal judge temporarily blocked Trump's limits on how much certain graduate students can borrow. That policy was set to go into effect July 1.

item.title: Core inflation rate hit 3.4% in May, highest since October 2023, Fed’s preferred gauge shows
item.description: The personal consumption expenditures price index was expected to show a 4.1% annual increase.

item.title: Bond ETF flows surge in hunt for yield: 'Market sniffing out something here,' says BlackRock exec
item.description: Bond investors are abandoning aggregate benchmarks in favor of a broad mix of fixed-income investments to maximize yield with the stock market on edge.

item.title: Wendy's turns lower as meme rally fails to extend to a second day
item.description: The stock's volatile run appeared largely disconnected from company fundamentals and instead reflected a burst of social media enthusiasm.

item.title: Supreme Court lets Trump end deportation protections for Syrians and Haitians
item.description: TPS allows migrants from countries stricken by war, natural disaster or other catastrophes to live and work in the U.S. while it's unsafe to return to their home countries.

item.title: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative's Biohub to open new rare disease funding round
item.description: Chan Zuckerberg Initiative's Biohub will offer new grants to the rare disease community while expanding its AI drug repurposing partnership with Every Cure.
